mainTopic: (continues) אין בו או אין בחומשו · חומש מעכב או אינו מעכב · חילול חומש על קרקע ואסימון
overview: (1) The Gemara discusses the definition of 'less than a prutah' regarding Ma'aser Sheni, debating whether it refers to the principal or the fifth, and calculates the Chomesh. (2) The Gemara debates whether the Chomesh is a requirement for valid redemption, analyzing the dispute between Rebbi Eliezer and Rebbi Yehoshua regarding eating after paying only the principal. (3) The Gemara discusses whether the Chomesh can be redeemed on land or non-coins, analyzing the laws of Hekdesh and Terumah, and concludes that the Chomesh follows the laws of the principal.

sugyaBoundaries.gist: The Gemara discusses the definition of 'less than a prutah' regarding Ma'aser Sheni, debating whether it refers to the principal or the fifth, and calculates the Chomesh.
The Gemara debates whether the Chomesh is a requirement for valid redemption, analyzing the dispute between Rebbi Eliezer and Rebbi Yehoshua regarding eating after paying only the principal.
The Gemara discusses whether the Chomesh can be redeemed on land or non-coins, analyzing the laws of Hekdesh and Terumah, and concludes that the Chomesh follows the laws of the principal.

steps.title: The תנאי on calculating חומש
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara introduces a תנאי dispute between רבי יוסי and רבי יהודה on whether the חומש is calculated from within or from without the principal.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya opens with a תנאי establishing the baseline dispute on how to calculate the חומש. This is a setup line introducing the two opposing calculation methods.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita quotes Leviticus 27:27. רבי יוסי holds the חומש is added to the principal (from without), while רבי יהודה holds it is taken from the principal (from within). This sets the stage for the subsequent dilemma about whether the חומש is a requirement for redemption.
steps.whatToRemember: The dispute is about calculation method, not yet about whether it מעכב.
steps.confusionAlert: Note the names: רבי יוסי and רבי יהודה, not רבי אליעזר and רבי יהושע yet.
steps.whyThisMatters: This תנאי is the starting point for the entire sugya's exploration of the חומש's status.

phrases.english: As the tanna'im: 'And shall add unto it one-fifth part thereof'
That it and its one-fifth be five
The words of Rabbi Yoshiya.

phrases.aramaic: רַבִּי יוֹנָתָן אוֹמֵר: חֲמִישִׁיתוֹ חוּמְשׁוֹ שֶׁל קֶרֶן.
phrases.english: Rabbi Yonatan says: Its one-fifth, its one-fifth of the principal.

steps.triggerLanguage: כְּתַנָּאֵי: ״וְיָסַף חֲמִשִּׁיתוֹ עָלָיו״, שֶׁיְּהֵא הוּא וְחוּמְשׁוֹ חֲמִשָּׁה, דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי יוֹסִי. רַבִּי יְהוּדָה אוֹמֵר: חֲמִשִּׁיתוֹ חוּמְשׁוֹ שֶׁל קֶרֶן.

rashi.hebrew: הוא וחומשו חמשה - היינו חומש מלבר שהוא רביע של קרן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבי יוסי's calculation of 'הוא וחומשו חמשה' means the חומש is added from outside the principal, constituting a quarter of the principal amount.

steps.title: The dilemma: Does חומש מעכב?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara raises a dilemma about whether the חומש is a requirement for valid redemption of מעשר שני.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara poses a fundamental question about the status of the חומש. This is a setup line introducing the central dilemma of the sugya.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The question is whether the חומש מעכב (prevents consumption if not paid). The Gemara explores two possibilities: either one can redeem with just the principal and add the חומש later (חומש לא מעכב), or one must pay the full amount including the חומש at the time of redemption (חומש מעכב).
steps.whatToRemember: This is the central question of the sugya.
steps.confusionAlert: The dilemma is phrased in terms of redemption amounts: 4 for 4 vs 4 for 5.
steps.whyThisMatters: The resolution of this dilemma determines the practical halacha of redeeming מעשר שני.

phrases.aramaic: אִיבַּעְיָא לְהוּ: חוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב, אוֹ אֵינוֹ מְעַכֵּב?
phrases.english: A dilemma was raised before them: Does the fifth prevent, or does it not prevent?
Four by four he redeems,
and upon himself he adds a fifth,
Apparently the fifth does not prevent.

phrases.aramaic: אוֹ דִּלְמָא אַרְבְּעָה בְּחַמְשָׁה פָּרֵיק, וְחוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב.
phrases.english: Or perhaps four by five he redeems, and the fifth prevents.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִיבַּעְיָא לְהוּ: חוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב, אוֹ אֵינוֹ מְעַכֵּב? אַרְבַּעַת בְּאַרְבַּעַת פָּרֵק, וְאַכְנַפְשֵׁיהּ מוֹסִיף חוֹמֶשׁ, אַלְמָא חוֹמֶשׁ לָא מְעַכֵּב. אוֹ דִּלְמָא אַרְבַּעַת בְּחָמֵשׁ פָּרֵק, וְחוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב.

rashi.hebrew: חומש מעכב - אכילה חוץ לחומה עד שיתן החומש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the question of whether חומש מעכב refers to whether one may eat the produce outside the walls of Jerusalem before paying the חומש.

rashi.hebrew: אכנפשיה - מאיליו אינו בכלל הפדיון שארבעה הזוזים פדו מעשר שהוא כנגדם:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that when the Gemara says one adds the חומש on himself, it means the חומש is not part of the redemption value itself, as the four coins redeem the מעשר that corresponds to them.

rashi.hebrew: או דלמא ארבעה בחמשה פריק - כך הוא גזירת הכתוב שהחומש מן הפדיון הוא ואין שוה ארבעה נפדין בפחות מחמשה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains the alternative view that one must redeem with five coins because the Torah decrees that the חומש is part of the redemption value, meaning produce worth four cannot be redeemed for less than five.

tosafot.hebrew: או דלמא ד' בה' פריק וחומש מעכב - וא"ת והלא בהקדש שוה מנה שחללו על שוה פרוטה מחולל ומסתמא הוא הדין במעשר וי"ל דודאי אם אמר הוא וחומשו מחולל על פרוטה (פחות) מועיל אבל כי לא מזכיר בהדיא החומש לא מהני:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות raises a difficulty from the law of הקדש, where one can consecrate an item worth a maneh on an item worth a prutah, suggesting the same should apply to מעשר, and answers that this works only if one explicitly states 'it and its fifth,' but not if the חומש is not explicitly mentioned.

steps.title: Ravina's proof from דמאי
steps.stepSummary: Ravina brings a proof from the laws of דמאי to resolve the dilemma.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּיןָא cites a Mishna about דמאי to show that the חומש is not a requirement. This is a proof line attempting to resolve the dilemma.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Mishna states that for דמאי, there is no חומש and no ביטול. רַבִּיןָא uses this to argue that since the חומש is not required for דמאי (which is only rabbinic), it must be that the חומש is not a requirement even for Torah-level מעשר שני. If it were a requirement, it would apply to דמאי as well.
steps.whatToRemember: The proof is from the absence of חומש in דמאי.
steps.confusionAlert: The logic is: if חומש מעכב, it should apply to דמאי too. Since it doesn't, it must not מעכב.
steps.whyThisMatters: This proof seems to resolve the dilemma in favor of חומש לא מעכב.

phrases.english: Ravina said: Come and hear:
Demai – it has no one-fifth,
and it has no eradication.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַבִּינָא, תָּא שְׁמַע: הַדְּמַאי – אֵין לוֹ חוֹמֶשׁ, וְאֵין לוֹ בִּטּוּל.

rashi.hebrew: הדמאי - מעשר שני של דמאי שחייבו חכמים להפריש הלוקח מעם הארץ:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that דמאי refers to מעשר שני that חכמים obligated to separate from produce purchased from an עמא הארץ.

rashi.hebrew: אין לו חומש - אם פודהו בעליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the statement 'אין לו חומש' applies when the owner redeems the מעשר שני.

rashi.hebrew: ואין לו ביעור - בשנה שלישית שנצטוו ישראל לבער כל מעשרותיהם מן הבית ולתת למי שהם רוצים כדכתיב (דברים יד) מקצה שלש שנים וגו':
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'אין לו ביעור' refers to the third year when ישראל are commanded to remove all their מעשרות from their homes and give them to whoever they wish, as written in דברים יד.

steps.title: Explaining the proof: קרן מעכב, חומש לא מעכב
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara explains Ravina's proof: the קרן is required even for דמאי, but the חומש is not, showing that the חומש is not a requirement.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara elaborates on רַבִּיןָא's proof, showing the logic behind it. This is an explanation line clarifying the previous step.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Gemara explains that the קֶרֶן is required for דמאי because it is a Torah requirement for regular מעשר שני. Since it is a Torah requirement, it applies to דמאי as well. However, the חומש is not required for דמאי because it is not a Torah requirement for regular מעשר שני. This shows that the חומש is not a requirement even for regular מעשר שני.
steps.whatToRemember: The logic is: Torah requirements apply to דמאי, rabbinic ones don't. Since חומש doesn't apply to דמאי, it must be rabbinic, not a Torah requirement.
steps.confusionAlert: The term 'מעכב' here means 'is a requirement for valid redemption'.
steps.whyThisMatters: This explanation solidifies the proof that חומש לא מעכב.

phrases.aramaic: הָא קֶרֶן יֵשׁ לוֹ. מַאי טַעְמָא?
phrases.english: Here, there is principal. What is the reason?
Principal, which prevents [consumption] by Torah law — it exists by rabbinic law,

phrases.aramaic: חוֹמֶשׁ דְּלָא מְעַכֵּב בִּדְאוֹרָיְיתָא – לֵיתַהּ בִּדְרַבָּנַן.
phrases.english: One-fifth, which does not prevent [consumption] by Torah law — it does not exist by rabbinic law.

steps.triggerLanguage: הָא קֶרֶן יֵשׁ לוֹ. מַאי טַעְמָא? קֶרֶן דִּמְעַכֵּב בִּדְאוֹרַיְתָא – אִיתָּהּ בִּדְרַבָּנָן, חוֹמֶשׁ דְּלָא מְעַכֵּב בִּדְאוֹרַיְתָא – לֵיתָּהּ בִּדְרַבָּנָן.

rashi.hebrew: הא קרן יש לו - דכל זמן שלא פדאו אסור לאוכלו חוץ לחומה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the קרן is considered 'his' because, until redemption, the produce remains forbidden to be eaten outside the walls of Jerusalem.

rashi.hebrew: מ"ט - אין לו חומש ויש לו קרן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the reason the קרן is retained while the חומש is not is that the קרן is a Torah-level obligation that carries over to rabbinic decrees, whereas the חומש is not.

rashi.hebrew: דמעכב מדאורייתא - במעשר דאורייתא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י specifies that 'מעכב מדאורייתא' refers to the status of the קרן in regular מעשר שני which is a Torah obligation.

rashi.hebrew: איתיה בדרבנן - במעשר דמאי דרבנן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that 'איתיה בדרבנן' means the obligation of the קרן applies to מעשר דמאי, which is a rabbinic enactment.

steps.title: Challenge from the dispute of רבי אליעזר and רבי יהושע
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara challenges the conclusion that חומש לא מעכב by citing a dispute between רבי אליעזר and רבי יהושע.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara brings a baraita that seems to contradict the conclusion that חומש לא מעכב. This is a challenge line introducing a new difficulty.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The baraita states that if one pays the קֶרֶן but not the חומש, רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר says one may eat, but רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ says one may not eat. This seems to imply that רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ holds that the חומש מעכב, contradicting the previous conclusion. The Gemara is pressing on the apparent contradiction.
steps.whatToRemember: The challenge is from a baraita that seems to show רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ holds חומש מעכב.
steps.confusionAlert: The baraita also mentions רבי's statement about שבת and חול, which complicates the picture.
steps.whyThisMatters: This challenge forces the Gemara to reconsider the conclusion that חומש לא מעכב.

phrases.english: Let us say that the Tannaim: He gave the principal,
and did not give the fifth,
Rabbi Eliezer says: He may eat.

phrases.aramaic: רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ אוֹמֵר: לֹא יֹאכַל. אָמַר רַבִּי:
phrases.english: Rabbi Yehoshua says: He may not eat. Rabbi said:
The words of Rabbi Eliezer appear correct on Shabbat,
and the words of Rabbi Yehoshua on the weekday.

steps.triggerLanguage: לֵימָא כְּתַנָּאֵי: נָתַן אֶת הַקֶּרֶן, וְלֹא נָתַן אֶת הַחוֹמֶשׁ, רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר אוֹמֵר: יֹאכַל. רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ אוֹמֵר: לֹא יֹאכַל. אָמַר רַבִּי: נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר בַּשַּׁבָּת, וְדִבְרֵי רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ בַּחֹל.

rashi.hebrew: בשבת - מפני כבוד שבת לא יעכב מלאכול בשביל חומש:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבי's statement that רבי אליעזר's words are preferable on שבת is because the honor of שבת overrides the requirement to pay the חומש before eating.

tosafot.hebrew: אמר רבי נראין כו' - סבר חומש לא מעכב ולכך בשבת חילל אבל בחול לא חילל דחיישינן לפשיעה ובשבת לא חיישינן משום עונג:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that רבי holds חומש לא מעכב, so on שבת one may desecrate the money for the sake of עונג שבת, but on חול one may not eat because we are concerned about פשיעה.

meforshim.interplaySummary: רש״י focuses on כבוד שבת as the reason רבי אליעזר's view is preferred on שבת, while תוספות explains the underlying logic that רבי holds חומש לא מעכב, allowing desecration for עונג שבת but not on חול due to פשיעה.

steps.title: Inferring a fundamental dispute from רבי's statement
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara infers from רבי's statement that רבי אליעזר and רבי יהושע disagree fundamentally, even on weekdays and Shabbat.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara analyzes רבי's statement to show that the dispute is fundamental. This is an analysis line drawing an inference from the text.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רבי says that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view is correct on Shabbat, and רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ's view is correct on weekdays. The Gemara infers that if they only agreed on weekdays, רבי wouldn't need to specify that רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ's view is correct on weekdays. Similarly, if they only agreed on Shabbat, רבי wouldn't need to specify that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view is correct on Shabbat. Therefore, they must disagree on both Shabbat and weekdays.
steps.whatToRemember: The inference is from the need to specify both cases.
steps.confusionAlert: The logic is: if they agreed on one case, רבי wouldn't need to mention it.
steps.whyThisMatters: This inference sets up the question about the nature of their dispute.

phrases.aramaic: מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר בַּשַּׁבָּת,
phrases.english: From the fact that he says: The words of Rabbi Eliezer appear correct on Shabbat,
From the general rule that they disagree even on weekdays.

phrases.aramaic: מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ בַּחוֹל,
phrases.english: From the fact that he says: The words of Rabbi Yehoshua appear correct on weekdays,
From the general rule that they disagree even on Shabbat.

steps.triggerLanguage: מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר בַּשַּׁבָּת, מִכְלָל דִּפְלִיגִי אֲפִלּוּ בְּחֹל. מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ בַּחֹל, מִכְלָל דִּפְלִיגִי אֲפִלּוּ בְּשַׁבָּת.

rashi.hebrew: מדקאמר רבי נראין דברי ר' אליעזר בשבת - אבל בחול אינו רואה את דבריו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רבי's statement that רבי אליעזer's words are correct on שבת implies that רבי does not accept רבי אליעזer's view on חול.

rashi.hebrew: מכלל דאיהו אפילו בחול - אמר יאכל ובמעשר ודאי קאמר:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the Gemara's inference that they disagree even on חול means that רבי holds the food should be eaten, and this ruling applies specifically to מעשר ודאי.

steps.title: What is the nature of their dispute?
steps.stepSummary: The Gemara asks whether the dispute is about whether the חומש מעכב.
steps.whatsHappening: The Gemara poses a question about the nature of the dispute between רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר and רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ. This is a setup line introducing a new question.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Gemara asks if the dispute is about whether the חומש מעכב. This is a neutral question, not an objection. The Gemara is exploring the possible explanations for the dispute.
steps.whatToRemember: This is a question, not a challenge.
steps.confusionAlert: The question is phrased as 'מַאי לָאו בְּהָא סְבָרָא קָמִיפְּלִיגִי'.
steps.whyThisMatters: This question leads to the final resolution of the sugya.

phrases.english: What, is it not that they disagree with regard to this reasoning:

phrases.aramaic: דְּרַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר סָבַר חוֹמֶשׁ לָא מְעַכֵּב,
phrases.english: That Rabbi Eliezer holds that the one-fifth does not prevent,
And Rabbi Yehoshua holds that the one-fifth prevents?

steps.triggerLanguage: מַאי לָאו בְּהָא סְבָרָא קָמִיפְּלִיגִי: דְּרַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר סָבַר חוֹמֶשׁ לָא מְעַכֵּב, וְרַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ סָבַר חוֹמֶשׁ מְעַכֵּב?

steps.title: Rav Pappa's resolution: Dispute about פשיעותא
steps.stepSummary: Rav Pappa resolves the dilemma by stating that everyone agrees the חומש does not מעכב, and the dispute is about whether we are concerned for negligence.
steps.whatsHappening: רַב פָּפָּא provides the final resolution to the sugya. This is a resolution line answering the previous question.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַב פָּפָּא states that everyone agrees the חומש does not מעכב. The dispute between רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר and רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ is about whether we are concerned for negligence (פְּשִׁיעוּתָא). רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ is concerned that people might forget to pay the חומש, so he says they may not eat until they do. רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר is not concerned for negligence, so he says they may eat even without paying the חומש.
steps.whatToRemember: The resolution is that the dispute is about פְּשִׁיעוּתָא, not about whether the חומש מעכב.
steps.confusionAlert: The term 'פְּשִׁיעוּתָא' means negligence or carelessness.
steps.whyThisMatters: This resolution reconciles the baraita with the earlier conclusion that חומש לא מעכב.

phrases.english: Rav Pappa said: No,
everyone in the world, a fifth does not prevent.
And here, in fearing negligence, they divided.
One Sage holds: we fear negligence.
And one Sage holds: we do not fear negligence.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַב פָּפָּא: לָא, דְּכוּלֵּי עָלְמָא חוֹמֶשׁ לָא מְעַכֵּב. וְהָכָא בְּחָיְשִׁינַן לִפְשִׁיעוּתָא קָמִיפְּלִיגִי. מָר סָבַר חָיְשִׁינַן לִפְשִׁיעוּתָא. וּמַר סָבַר לָא חָיְשִׁינַן לִפְשִׁיעוּתָא.

rashi.hebrew: דכ"ע חומש לא מעכב - שיהא האוכלו לוקה עליו משום לא תוכל לאכול בשעריך (שם יב):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the statement that everyone agrees חומש does not מעכב means that one who eats the produce without paying the חומש is not liable for lashes based on the verse לא תוכל לאכול בשעריך.

rashi.hebrew: והכא בחוששין לפשיעותא - הא דקאמר רבי יהושע לא יאכל מדרבנן קאמר שמא יפשע ויתיאש ולא יתן חומש עוד:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that רבי יהושע's prohibition to eat is a rabbinic decree due to concern for פשיעותא, fearing the person might become negligent, lose hope, and cease paying the חומש entirely.

steps.title: רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן: הַכּוֹל מוֹדִים בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ שֶׁחִילֵּל
steps.stepSummary: רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן states that all agree that in the case of הֶקְדֵּשׁ, if one pays the principal but not the חוֹמֵשׁ, the redemption is valid because the גִּזְבָּרִים will demand payment later.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya opens with a רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's teaching establishing a baseline agreement regarding הֶקְדֵּשׁ.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן asserts that despite disputes elsewhere, there is universal agreement that redeeming הֶקְדֵּשׁ without the חוֹמֵשׁ is effective. The rationale is that the Temple treasurers (גִּזְבָּרִים) will collect the חוֹמֵשׁ later in the marketplace, so the initial redemption stands.

keyTerms.meaning: the additional one-fifth required for redemption

steps.whatToRemember: The key is that the חוֹמֵשׁ is not required for the initial redemption of הֶקְדֵּשׁ to be valid.
steps.confusionAlert: This seems to contradict a later בְּרִיתָא, which is the point of the next step.
steps.whyThisMatters: This sets up the central question of the sugya: does the חוֹמֵשׁ follow the laws of the principal, or does it have independent restrictions?

phrases.english: Rabbi Yoḥanan says:
All concede regarding consecrated property — that he desacralized it,
since the treasurers demand it from him in the marketplace.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן: הַכּוֹל מוֹדִים בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ – שֶׁחִילֵּל, הוֹאִיל וְגִזְבָּרִים תּוֹבְעִים אוֹתוֹ בַּשּׁוּק.

rashi.hebrew: הואיל וגזברין תובעין אותו - את החומש וליכא למיחש לפשיעותא:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the גִּזְבָּרִים will pursue the חוֹמֵשׁ separately, so there is no concern for פְּשִׁיעָה (negligence) in the initial redemption.

steps.title: Challenge from a בְּרִיתָא about רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר and חֲכָמִים
steps.stepSummary: The גְּמָרָא challenges רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's claim of universal agreement by citing a בְּרִיתָא where רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר and חֲכָמִים disagree about redeeming הֶקְדֵּשׁ without the חוֹמֵשׁ.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא attacks the opening מימרא with a contradictory source.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The בְּרִיתָא states that if one pays the principal but not the חוֹמֵשׁ, רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר says the redemption is valid, but חֲכָמִים say it is not. רַבִּי (רַבִּי יְהוּדָה הַנָּשִׂיא) clarifies that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view applies to הֶקְדֵּשׁ and חֲכָמִים's view applies to מַעֲשֵׂר. This implies a dispute even in הֶקְדֵּשׁ, contradicting רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן.

keyTerms.meaning: a Tannaitic source outside the Mishna
a Tanna who holds leniently on redemption

steps.whatToRemember: The בְּרִיתָא shows a dispute in הֶקְדֵּשׁ, which directly contradicts the opening claim.
steps.confusionAlert: The בְּרִיתָא seems to split the dispute between הֶקְדֵּשׁ and מַעֲשֵׂר, but the inference is that they disagree in both.
steps.whyThisMatters: This forces a re-evaluation of רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's statement.

phrases.english: And do they not disagree with regard to consecrated property? But isn't it taught:
He gave the principal,
and did not give him the additional one-fifth,
Rabbi Eliezer says:
He desacralized, and the Rabbis say:
He did not desacralize. Rabbi said:
The words of Rabbi Eliezer appear correct with regard to consecrated property,
and the words of the Rabbis with regard to second tithe.

steps.triggerLanguage: וּבְהֶקְדֵּשׁ לָא פְּלִיגִי? וְהָתַנְיָא: נָתַן אֶת הַקֶּרֶן, וְלֹא נָתַן לוֹ אֶת הַחוֹמֵשׁ, רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר אוֹמֵר: חִילֵּל, וַחֲכָמִים אוֹמְרִים: לֹא חִילֵּל.

steps.title: Inference that the dispute exists in both הֶקְדֵּשׁ and מַעֲשֵׂר
steps.stepSummary: The גְּמָרָא infers from רַבִּי's clarification that the dispute exists in both הֶקְדֵּשׁ and מַעֲשֵׂר, further contradicting רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא deepens the challenge by analyzing the implications of רַבִּי's statement.
steps.deeperAnalysis: If רַבִּי specifies that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view applies to הֶקְדֵּשׁ, it implies they also disagree in מַעֲשֵׂר. Similarly, if חֲכָמִים's view applies to מַעֲשֵׂר, it implies they disagree in הֶקְדֵּשׁ. This means there is a dispute in both areas, directly contradicting רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's claim of universal agreement in הֶקְדֵּשׁ.
steps.whatToRemember: The inference is that the dispute is not limited to one area but exists in both.
steps.confusionAlert: This is a logical inference from the wording of רַבִּי's statement.
steps.whyThisMatters: This forces a resolution of the contradiction with רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן.

phrases.aramaic: מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ –
phrases.english: From the fact that he said the words of Rabbi Eliezer appear correct in consecrated property –
From the general rule that they disagree even in second tithe.
From the fact that he said the words of the Sages appear correct in second…
From the general rule that they disagree even in consecrated property!

steps.triggerLanguage: מִדְּאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ – מִכְלָל דְּפְלִיג אֲפִלּוּ בְּמַעֲשֵׂר. מִדְּקָאָמַר נִרְאִין דִּבְרֵי חֲכָמִים בְּמַעֲשֵׂר – מִכְלָל דְּאִינְהוּ פְּלִיגִי אֲפִלּוּ בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ!

rashi.hebrew: נראין דברי רבי אליעזר בהקדש - הואיל וגזברין תובעין אותו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view is considered 'נראין' in הֶקְדֵּשׁ because the גזברין actively pursue the funds, making the case more stringent.

tosafot.hebrew: מכלל דפליג אפילו בהקדש - תימה לישני דה"ק נראין דברי ר' אליעזר לר' יהושע בהקדש שאף ר' יהושע לא פליג אלא במעשר דהכי משני בפ"ק דחולין (דף יב.) ואי גרסינן הכא תרי זימנין נראין ונראין אתי שפיר ולספרים דגרסינן נתן לו מוכח בהדיא דמיירי בהקדש דנתן לו משמע לגזבר:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions the Gemara's inference that רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר disagrees in הֶקְדֵּשׁ, suggesting the text means רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ agrees with him in הֶקְדֵּשׁ but only disagrees in מַעֲשֵׂר, as explained in חולין, and notes that manuscripts reading 'נתן לו' clearly indicate the case involves הֶקְדֵּשׁ given to a גזבר.

meforshim.interplaySummary: תוספות challenges the Gemara's inference that the dispute extends to הֶקְדֵּשׁ, proposing an alternative reading where רַבִּי יְהוֹשֻׁעַ agrees in הֶקְדֵּשׁ, whereas רש״י accepts the Gemara's inference and explains why רַבִּי אֱלִיעֶזֶר's view is preferred in הֶקְדֵּשׁ due to the active pursuit by גזברין.

steps.title: רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's statement refers to שַׁבָּת
steps.stepSummary: The גְּמָרָא resolves the contradiction by reinterpreting רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's statement as referring to redemption on שַׁבָּת, where there is universal agreement.
steps.whatsHappening: The גְּמָרָא offers a resolution to the contradiction by reinterpreting the opening מימרא.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גְּמָרָא suggests that רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן was not speaking about general redemption, but specifically about redemption on שַׁבָּת. In this case, all agree that the redemption is valid even without the חוֹמֵשׁ, because the verse says 'וְקָרָאתָ לַשַּׁבָּת עֹנֶג' (you shall call Shabbat a delight), and the Sages wanted to facilitate this delight. Additionally, the גִּזְבָּרִים will collect the חוֹמֵשׁ later.

keyTerms.meaning: if it was stated, it was stated like this

steps.whatToRemember: The resolution is that רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן's statement is limited to שַׁבָּת.
steps.confusionAlert: This is a classic 'אִלּוּ אִתְּמַר הָכִי אִתְּמַר' resolution.
steps.whyThisMatters: This resolves the contradiction and allows the sugya to move forward.

phrases.english: Rather, if it was stated, it was stated like this:
Rabbi Yoḥanan says:
All concede concerning Shabbat with regard to consecrated property that he successfully desacralized.
One reason — as it is written: 'And you shall call Shabbat a delight'.

phrases.aramaic: וְעוֹד, הוֹאִיל וְגִזְבָּרִין תּוֹבְעִין אוֹתוֹ בַּשּׁוּק.
phrases.english: And furthermore, since the treasurers demand payment from him in the marketplace.

steps.triggerLanguage: אֶלָּא אִי אִתְּמַר, הָכִי אִתְּמַר: אָמַר רַבִּי יוֹחָנָן: הַכּוֹל מוֹדִים בְּשַׁבָּת בְּהֶקְדֵּשׁ שֶׁחִילֵּל.

steps.title: רָמֵי בַּר חָמָא: הֶקְדֵּשׁ אֵינוֹ מִתְחַלֵּל עַל הַקַּרְקַע
steps.stepSummary: רָמֵי בַּר חָמָא states that הֶקְדֵּשׁ cannot be redeemed with land, based on the verse 'וְנָתַן הַכֶּסֶף וְקָם לוֹ'.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya transitions to a new question about the redemption of הֶקְדֵּשׁ with land.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רָמֵי בַּר חָמָא cites the Sages' ruling that הֶקְדֵּשׁ cannot be redeemed with land. The source is the verse 'וְנָתַן הַכֶּסֶף וְקָם לוֹ' (and he will give the money and it will be assured to him), which implies that redemption must be with money, not land. The question is whether the חוֹמֵשׁ can be paid with land.

keyTerms.meaning: and he will give the money and it will be assured to him

steps.whatToRemember: The principal cannot be redeemed with land, but the question is about the חוֹמֵשׁ.
steps.confusionAlert: This is a new question, not a continuation of the previous one.
steps.whyThisMatters: This sets up the central question of the sugya: does the חוֹמֵשׁ follow the laws of the principal?

phrases.english: Rami bar Ḥama says:

phrases.aramaic: הֲרֵי אָמְרוּ הֶקְדֵּשׁ אֵינוֹ מִתְחַלֵּל עַל הַקַּרְקַע,
phrases.english: Behold, they said: consecrated property is not desacralized upon the land,
The Merciful One said: 'And he shall give the silver and it shall stand to…
Its fifth — what is it that it shall be desacralized upon the land?

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רָמֵי בַּר חָמָא: הֲרֵי אָמְרוּ הֶקְדֵּשׁ אֵינוֹ מִתְחַלֵּל עַל הַקַּרְקַע, דְּרַחְמָנָא אָמַר: ״וְנָתַן הַכֶּסֶף וְקָם לוֹ״. חוּמְשׁוֹ מַהוּ שֶׁיִּתְחַלֵּל עַל הַקַּרְקַע?

rashi.hebrew: הרי אמרו - במסכת בכורות בפרק בתרא (ד' נא.):
rashi.takeaway: רש״י identifies the source of the ruling that הֶקְדֵּשׁ cannot be redeemed with land as being in מסכת בכורות, פרק בתרא.

rashi.hebrew: ונתן הכסף - ואם המקדיש יגאל וגומר ויסף חמישית כסף ערכך עליו והיה לו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the verse 'וְנָתַן הַכֶּסֶף' teaches that redemption requires money, and the חוֹמֵשׁ must also be paid in money as indicated by 'וְיֹסֵף חֲמִישִׁית כֶּסֶף עַל עֶרְכְּךָ'.

tosafot.hebrew: הקדש אינו מתחלל על הקרקע - הא דאמר שמואל לקמן (בבא מציעא דף נז:) בונין בחול ואח"כ מקדישין למ"ד (חולין דף טז.) תלוש ולבסוף חיברו הוי תלוש אתי שפיר ואפי' למ"ד חשוב כמחובר מ"מ כיון דאם לא היה קדוש סופו ליעקר מקרי תלוש:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות reconciles the rule that הֶקְדֵּשׁ cannot be redeemed with land with שמואל's statement that one can build with חול materials and then consecrate them, by explaining that even if the materials become important as attached, they are still considered תלוש because they would eventually be uprooted if not consecrated.

tosafot.hebrew: ונתן הכסף וקם לו - וא"ת אי כסף דוקא מטלטלין נמי לא ואי גמרי' מנזקין ועבד דכתיב בהן (שמות כא) ישיב לרבות שוה כסף ככסף א"כ קרקע נמי וי"ל דדרשינן בכלל ופרט ויסף חמישית כלל כסף פרט והיה לו חזר וכלל וכן משמע פ' יש בכור (בכורות ד' נא.) דתנן אין פודין את הבכור בקרקעות כו' ולא בהקדשות ומפרש בגמ' ולא הקדשות בכל אלו והתם גבי בכור דריש בכלל ופרט וכלל וא"ת דאי לא הוה כתב אלא כסף לחודיה ה"א שוה כסף ככסף והשתא דכתיבי כללי ממעטינן קרקעות וי"ל דהכא כסף מיותר דה"מ למכתב חמישית ערכך וגבי בכור נמי ופדויו מבן חדש תפדה בערכך ה' שקלים ומדכתיב כסף למדרשיה לפרטא הוא דאתא:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות explains that the exclusion of land from redemption is derived through a כלל ופרט וכלל structure in the verse, where 'כסף' is the פרט that excludes land, and this same method is used in מסכת בכורות to exclude land from redeeming a בכור.

steps.title: תְּרוּמָה אֵינָהּ מִשְׁתַּלֶּמֶת אֶלָּא מִן הַחוּלִין
steps.stepSummary: The גְּמָרָא states that תְּרוּמָה can only be repaid with חוּלִין, based on the verse 'וְנָתַן לַכֹּהֵן אֶת הַקֹּדֶשׁ'.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya presents a parallel case regarding תְּרוּמָה.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גְּמָרָא states that תְּרוּמָה can only be repaid with חוּלִין (non-sacred produce), not with תְּרוּמָה. The source is the verse 'וְנָתַן לַכֹּהֵן אֶת הַקֹּדֶשׁ' (and he shall give to the priest that which is sacred), which implies that the repayment must be with something that can become sacred, i.e., חוּלִין. The question is whether the חוֹמֵשׁ can be paid with תְּרוּמָה.

keyTerms.meaning: and he shall give to the priest that which is sacred

steps.whatToRemember: The principal must be paid with חוּלִין, but the question is about the חוֹמֵשׁ.
steps.confusionAlert: This is a parallel case to the previous one.
steps.whyThisMatters: This reinforces the central question of the sugya.

phrases.aramaic: תְּרוּמָה אֵינָהּ מִשְׁתַּלֶּמֶת אֶלָּא מִן הַחוּלִּין.
phrases.english: Teruma is not repaid except from non-sacred property.
The Merciful One said: 'And he shall give to the priest the sacred,'
A matter fit to be sacred.

phrases.aramaic: חוּמְשָׁהּ מַהוּ שֶׁיִּשְׁתַּלֵּם שֶׁלֹּא מִן הַחוּלִּין?
phrases.english: Its one-fifth — what is the law that it be repaid not from non-sacred property?

steps.triggerLanguage: תְּרוּמָה אֵינָהּ מִשְׁתַּלֶּמֶת אֶלָּא מִן הַחוּלִין. דְּרַחְמָנָא אָמַר: ״וְנָתַן לַכֹּהֵן אֶת הַקֹּדֶשׁ״, דָּבָר הָרָאוּי לִהְיוֹת קֹדֶשׁ. חוּמְשָׁהּ מַהוּ שֶׁיִּשְׁתַּלֵּם שֶׁלֹּא מִן הַחוּלִין?

rashi.hebrew: תרומה - זר האוכלה בשוגג:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the subject of the discussion is a זר who ate תרומה בְּשׁוֹגֵג, establishing the context for the repayment obligation.

rashi.hebrew: אינה משתלמת - מעות אלא פירות חולין שהתשלומין נעשין תרומה כדכתיב בו את הקודש מה שהוא נותן לו נעשה קודש אלמא מידי דחזי להכי בעינן:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that repayment must be made with חולין produce rather than money, because the verse 'וְנָתַן לַכֹּהֵן אֶת הַקֹּדֶשׁ' requires the item given to be capable of becoming קודש, which money cannot do.

rashi.hebrew: שלא מן החולין - מעות או בגדים שוה כסף:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the question regarding the חומש asks whether it can be paid with non-produce items like money or clothing worth a silver coin, rather than with חולין produce.

steps.title: מַעֲשֵׂר אֵין מִתְחַלֵּל עַל הָאֲסִימוֹן
steps.stepSummary: The גְּמָרָא states that מַעֲשֵׂר cannot be redeemed with an אֲסִימוֹן, based on the verse 'וְצַרְתָּ הַכֶּסֶף בְּיָדְךָ'.
steps.whatsHappening: The sugya presents a third parallel case regarding מַעֲשֵׂר.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The גְּמָרָא states that מַעֲשֵׂר cannot be redeemed with an אֲסִימוֹן (unminted coin). The source is the verse 'וְצַרְתָּ הַכֶּסֶף בְּיָדְךָ' (and you shall bind up the money in your hand), which implies that redemption must be with something that has a צוּרָה (imprint), i.e., a minted coin. The question is whether the חוֹמֵשׁ can be paid with an אֲסִימוֹן.

keyTerms.meaning: and you shall bind up the money in your hand

steps.whatToRemember: The principal must be paid with a minted coin, but the question is about the חוֹמֵשׁ.
steps.confusionAlert: This is a third parallel case.
steps.whyThisMatters: This further reinforces the central question of the sugya.

phrases.english: Second-tithe produce cannot be desacralized with an unminted coin.
The Merciful One states: "And you shall bind up the money in your hand,"

phrases.aramaic: לְרַבּוֹת כׇּל דָּבָר שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו צוּרָה.
phrases.english: To include any item that has an imprint upon it.
Its additional one-fifth — what is the law that it be desacralized with an unminted…

steps.triggerLanguage: מַעֲשֵׂר אֵין מִתְחַלֵּל עַל הָאֲסִימוֹן. דְּרַחְמָנָא אָמַר: ״וְצַרְתָּ הַכֶּסֶף בְּיָדְךָ״, לְרַבּוֹת כָּל דָּבָר שֶׁיֵּשׁ עָלָיו צוּרָה. חוּמְשׁוֹ מַהוּ שֶׁיִּתְחַלֵּל עַל הָאֲסִימוֹן?

rashi.hebrew: עליו - בכולהו חומשין כתיב יוסף עליו:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the verse 'וְצַרְתָּ הַכֶּסֶף בְּיָדְךָ' is derived from the word 'עָלָיו' which appears in all the verses regarding חוֹמֵשׁ, specifically 'יוֹסֵף עָלָיו', indicating the requirement for a specific form of payment.

steps.title: רַבָּא: 'עָלָיו' includes the חוֹמֵשׁ
steps.stepSummary: רַבָּא resolves the questions by stating that the verse 'עָלָיו' includes the חוֹמֵשׁ, meaning it follows the laws of the principal.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבָּא provides a resolution to the series of questions.
steps.deeperAnalysis: רַבָּא states that the verse 'עָלָיו' (upon it) includes the חוֹמֵשׁ, meaning that the חוֹמֵשׁ follows the laws of the principal. Therefore, just as the principal cannot be redeemed with land, תְּרוּמָה, or an אֲסִימוֹן, neither can the חוֹמֵשׁ.
steps.whatToRemember: The חוֹמֵשׁ follows the laws of the principal.
steps.confusionAlert: This is the central resolution of the sugya.
steps.whyThisMatters: This resolves the central question of the sugya.

phrases.english: The matter rolled and came before Rava.

phrases.aramaic: אֲמַר לְהוּ: אָמַר קְרָא ״עָלָיו״, לְרַבּוֹת חוּמְשׁוֹ כְּמוֹתוֹ.
phrases.english: He said to them: The verse says 'upon it', to include its one-fifth like it.

steps.triggerLanguage: אִתְגַּלְגַּל מִלְתָּא וּמְטָא לְקַמֵּיהּ דְּרַבָּא. אֲמַר לְהוּ: אָמַר קְרָא ״עָלָיו״, לְרַבּוֹת חוּמְשׁוֹ כְּמוֹתוֹ.

tosafot.hebrew: עליו לרבות שחומשו כמותו - וא"ת והלא אין חומש מעכב א"כ אין חומש כמותו ובסמוך גבי מעשר נמי אמרינן דאין מוסיף חומש על החומש וי"ל דעליו לא מיירי אלא בדבר שמשלם קרן משלם חומש:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות addresses the difficulty that if the חומש is truly like the principal, it should prevent redemption, yet we learn that the חומש does not prevent redemption and one does not add a חומש on the חומש, and resolves that the verse 'עליו' only applies to cases where one pays the principal, thereby obligating the חומש.

steps.title: Proof from the Mishna about תְּרוּמָה
steps.stepSummary: רַבִּינָא provides a proof from the Mishna that the חוֹמֵשׁ follows the laws of the principal.
steps.whatsHappening: רַבִּינָא cites a Mishna about stolen תְּרוּמָה to support רַבָּא's resolution. This is a setup line introducing a textual proof.
steps.deeperAnalysis: The Mishna distinguishes between paying for uneaten stolen תְּרוּמָה versus eaten stolen תְּרוּמָה. It specifies that the חוֹמֵשׁ is paid from חוּלִין, mirroring the principal's status. This confirms that the חוֹמֵשׁ follows the laws of the principal, exactly as רַבָּא argued.

phrases.aramaic: אָמַר רָבִינָא: אַף אֲנַן נָמֵי תְּנֵינָא,
phrases.english: Ravina said: We too learn
One who steals teruma and did not eat it –
Pays double payment of the value of teruma.
He ate it – he pays two principals and a fifth.

phrases.aramaic: קֶרֶן וָחוֹמֶשׁ מִן הַחוּלִּין, וְהַקֶּרֶן דְּמֵי תְרוּמָה.
phrases.english: A principal and a fifth from non-sacred items, and the principal [is] the value of teruma.

steps.triggerLanguage: אָמַר רַבִּינָא: אַף אֲנַן נָמֵי תַּנְיָא, הַגּוֹנֵב תְּרוּמָה וְלֹא אָכְלָהּ – מְשַׁלֵּם תַּשְׁלוּמֵי כֶּפֶל דְּמֵי תְּרוּמָה. אָכְלָהּ – מְשַׁלֵּם שְׁנֵי קְרָנִים וָחוֹמֵשׁ. קֶרֶן וָחוֹמֵשׁ מִן הַחוּלִין, וְהַקֶּרֶן דְּמֵי תְּרוּמָה.

rashi.hebrew: ולא אכלה - דהיא חוזרת בעין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that the case of 'not eating' the תְּרוּמָה refers to a situation where the stolen item remains intact and can be returned in its original form.

rashi.hebrew: משלם את הכפל - של קנס גניבה במעות:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the double payment (כֶּפֶל) for theft is paid in money, not in kind.

rashi.hebrew: דמי תרומה - כמה שהיא שוה לימכר בשוק ודמיה פחותין מדמי חולין שאינה ראויה אלא לכהנים ואינה ראויה בימי טומאתו ואם מטמאה אסורה באכילה לפיכך דמיה מועטים והיינו דקאמר דמי תרומה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'value of תְּרוּמָה' refers to its lower market price, which is less than חוּלִין because it is only fit for כֹּהֲנִים and becomes forbidden if the thief is טָמֵא.

rashi.hebrew: אכלה - בשוגג דלא ידע שהיא תרומה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י specifies that the case of 'eating' the תְּרוּמָה refers to an accidental consumption (בְּשִׁגְגָה) where the thief did not know it was תְּרוּמָה.

rashi.hebrew: שני קרנין - אחד לקרן ואחד לכפל וחומש בשביל אכילתה:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י breaks down the 'two principals' (שְׁנֵי קְרָנִים) as one principal for the theft itself and one principal for the double penalty, with the חוֹמֵשׁ added due to the consumption.

rashi.hebrew: מן החולין - פירות מתוקנין:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י explains that 'from חוּלִין' means the payment is made with regular, non-sacred produce.

rashi.hebrew: והקרן - השני שהוא בא מחמת כפל של קנס גניבה משלם נמי דמי תרומה כלומר מעות כמו שנמכרת תרומה בשוק:
rashi.takeaway: רש״י clarifies that the second principal, which stems from the double penalty for theft, is also paid according to the lower market value of תְּרוּמָה in money.

tosafot.hebrew: אף אנן נמי תנינא - ה"מ לאתויי מתני' דמייתי בסמוך דמשלם חומשא דחומשא וכיון שנעשה החומש תרומה א"כ משתלם מדבר הראוי להיות קודש ומיהו יש לדחות דמצי לשלם בכל דבר וכי משלם דבר הראוי להיות קודש אז נעשה תרומה אי נמי מהך דהכא עדיפא ליה לאתויי דקתני בהדיא חומש מן החולין:
tosafot.takeaway: תוספות questions why רַבִּינָא cites this Mishna, suggesting it might be to prove that the חוֹמֵשׁ becomes sacred and must be paid with items fit for קֹדֶשׁ, but counters that one can pay with anything and this Mishna is better cited because it explicitly states the חוֹמֵשׁ is paid from חוּלִין.
